_comment: MARB run registry + provenance manifest. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for which runs exist and their full version traceability. marb_grade_all.py --manifest reads this: it groups runs by `cell`, grades each `step`, aggregates per cell, and carries `toolchain` + per-seed provenance into marb_v0_9_stats.json. New runs: append an entry (drop the STEP anywhere and point `step` at it, or use the [local-path-redacted] convention). Nothing auto-populates this — a run is a human/agent CAD session; register it here.

grader_toolchain._comment: Versions of the GRADER stack (the product). Same for every run unless overridden per-run.
grader_toolchain.fea_solid_mesh: CalculiX 2.23.0 (not exercised by v0.9 positional metrics)

harness_environment._comment: Client + versions for the CURRENT cohort (kit v1.2, runs from 2026-05-30 on). Earlier runs differed: Opus 4.7 used Claude Code (CLI); the Opus 4.8 Fusion attempt used the Claude desktop app (incognito). Record per-run client/kit_version going forward.
harness_environment.client: Claude Code via the VS Code extension
harness_environment.ultracode: NOT used. As of 2026-05-30, ultracode is available only in the VS Code extension, not the Claude Code desktop app. Noted because it would materially change effort/token use; these runs are standard Max, no ultracode.

_pending_runs_template._comment: Copy an entry into runs[] when each new STEP lands. Fill driver.version for every tool so the contributing-versions axis stays complete.
_pending_runs_template.client: Claude Code via the VS Code extension (see harness_environment)
_pending_runs_template.blind_method: neutral folder, no CLAUDE.md, account memory not in play
